title: Elbow (Olecranon) Bursitis
license_notes: Use as source support only. Avoid verbatim copying from AAOS copyrighted patient education.
supports: Olecranon bursitis commonly presents with swelling at the back or tip of the elbow and may be infectious or noninfectious.
Redness, warmth, pain, pus, fever, or spreading infection concern requires separate evaluation and should not be routed through simple overuse elbow instructions.
Olecranon bursitis has a different home-care pathway than epicondylitis or elbow overuse strain and should be routed separately.
